name: Success Tree
description: Explain how multiple contributing factors led to a successful outcome
systemPromptHint: Identify causal factors, not events — "won enterprise deal" is an event; "deep domain expertise in vertical" is a factor. Apply counterfactual thinking: if the factor were absent, would the success still occur? If yes, remove it. Guard against hindsight bias: ask "would we have predicted this mattered beforehand?" Distinguish repeatable drivers from luck — only the former belong in the tree.

nodeTags.name: Success Factor
nodeTags.description: A condition, decision, or action that helped produce the outcome

nodeTags.name: Achievement
nodeTags.description: A positive outcome, milestone, or result worth explaining

derivedIndicators.name: Foundation
derivedIndicators.description: No incoming edges — a foundational contributor to the success

derivedIndicators.name: Contributor
derivedIndicators.description: Has both incoming and outgoing edges — an intermediate contributor

derivedIndicators.name: Outcome
derivedIndicators.description: No outgoing edges — a success or achievement being explained
